_readme: Edit this file to update your site content. Push to GitHub and Cloudflare Pages redeploys automatically. No code changes needed.
_categories: Science | Education | Politics | History | Agriculture | Urban Planning | Interdisciplinary | Policy

projects.title: Rediscovering Raleigh's Lost Freedmen Villages
projects.description: AP African American Studies research mapping the post-Civil War Freedmen settlements that built modern Raleigh.
projects.problem: Raleigh's post-emancipation Black communities — Oberlin, Method, Hayti — were systematically erased from the official record by 20th-century urban renewal. Their geography is missing from most local histories.
projects.impact: Reconstructs five lost villages using historic plats, census records, and oral histories. Now used as supplemental material in WCPSS African American Studies classrooms.

projects.title: Learning by Mapping: A New Kind of Curriculum
projects.description: Esri UC 2025 presentation — making the case for place-based GIS as a K-12 pedagogy to ~200 educators.
projects.problem: Geography is the most-cut subject in American K-12 education, yet spatial reasoning underlies every modern science. Few teachers have a curriculum that bridges the two.
projects.impact: Presented at the 2025 Esri User Conference. The framework now informs 4-H Geospatial Leadership Team curriculum across four states.

projects.title: Researching Raleigh's Neighborhoods and Communities
projects.description: GIS Day 2024 winning project — a block-by-block read of how Raleigh actually lives.
projects.problem: Raleigh's rapid growth has left residents without a public tool to understand how their own neighborhoods compare on access, demographics, and change over time.
projects.impact: Won the 2024 NC State GIS Day student competition. Cited by a Raleigh Planning Commission member as a model for civic spatial communication.

projects.title: Turning the tide for North Carolina's coastal communities
projects.description: Sea level rise, salt marsh loss, and the working communities on the front line of NC's coast.
projects.problem: NC's coastal counties are losing wetland buffers faster than nearly any state on the Atlantic — but the impact is mostly framed as ecological rather than economic and human.
projects.impact: Shared with the NC Coastal Federation as a communication template for community outreach.

projects.title: WCPSS Students Using GIS Collection
projects.description: A curated collection of student GIS work from across Wake County Public Schools.
projects.problem: Teachers need a visible body of student work to make the case for GIS curriculum to administrators — but there was no central place to point to.
projects.impact: Distributed to WCPSS GIS staff and teacher liaisons; the canonical example portfolio for the district's GIS instruction expansion.

projects.title: Behind CAPS MUN 2025: Building Model UN with GIS + AI
projects.description: The first GIS-integrated Model UN conference in the United States.
projects.problem: Model UN crises rely on dense briefing packets that delegates rarely fully read, and almost never ground-truth with maps. Spatial context is lost.
projects.impact: Delegates used live maps and AI-assisted research during crisis sessions. The format is being adopted by two other NC conferences in 2026.

projects.title: How GPS revolutionized marine science
projects.description: A history of how satellite positioning rewrote what marine scientists could ask.
projects.problem: Most students learn GPS as a navigation tool, missing the deeper story: pre-GPS oceanography was bottlenecked by the inability to know your location within a hundred meters.
projects.impact: Archived as part of the Geography At Work interdisciplinary collection.

projects.title: Geography At Work
projects.description: A collection showing how spatial thinking powers science, policy, ag, and history alike.
projects.problem: GIS is too often pitched as a single discipline. The real case for it is interdisciplinary — and that case isn't made well in any single project.
projects.impact: Used in 4-H Geospatial Leadership Team onboarding as the umbrella interdisciplinary collection.

projects.title: Growers across North Carolina
projects.description: NOURISH project — mapping the people behind NC's working farms.
projects.problem: NC ag policy is shaped at the state level, but conditions for growers vary enormously by county — a variation invisible at the policy level.
projects.impact: Cited by NC Ag Extension contacts as a useful frame for outreach. Core spatial layer of the NOURISH project.

in_progress.title: Supply Chain Analysis
in_progress.desc: Food supply chain mapping across NC agricultural counties — tracing inputs, outputs, and chokepoints.

in_progress.title: Medicare Fraud Mapped
in_progress.desc: Geographic fraud-pattern analysis across CMS billing data — locating anomalies that evade tabular audit.

in_progress.title: Sea Level Rise Impact
in_progress.desc: Extension of the coastal communities StoryMap with parcel-level inundation modeling under IPCC scenarios.

in_progress.title: Mega Ant War GIS Project
in_progress.desc: Invasive species spread tracking — mapping the front lines of fire ant vs. Argentine ant territory wars.

highlights.title: TEDx Talk
highlights.desc: Selected to give a TEDx talk on spatial thinking and civic technology. Update this entry with your full event name, talk title, and link.

highlights.title: Esri User Conference 2025
highlights.desc: Presented 'Learning by Mapping: A New Kind of Curriculum' to ~200 GIS educators at the world's largest GIS conference.

highlights.title: GIS Day 2024 Champion
highlights.desc: Won the NC State University GIS Day student competition for the Raleigh Neighborhoods & Communities StoryMap.

highlights.title: National 4-H Geospatial Leadership Team
highlights.desc: Lead the national team developing GIS curriculum and training programs for 4-H clubs across the United States.

about_items.title: The Map Company
about_items.desc: GIS consulting — civic analytics, ag systems, outreach visualization

about_items.title: National 4-H Geospatial Leadership Team
about_items.desc: Lead · curriculum development across four states

about_items.title: Raleigh, North Carolina
about_items.desc: Built most of these projects in Wake County

learning.title: Google Photorealistic 3D Basemap Now Available in ArcGIS Online
learning.take: Photorealistic 3D scenes in StoryMaps will change how community presentations feel — policy problems become tangible when you can walk through a neighborhood in 3D.

learning.title: The 15-Minute City: Network Analysis vs. Euclidean Distance in Walkability Scoring
learning.take: Using road network distance instead of straight-line radius makes walkability scores far more accurate — I need to update the scoring in my neighborhood analysis.

learning.title: Place-Based GIS Education Increases Spatial Retention by 38% in Middle School Students
learning.take: Connecting GIS to students' own neighborhoods — not abstract datasets — is the core finding, validating everything we've been doing with 4-H curriculum in Wake County.

learning.title: Food Desert Identification and Grocery Access Gaps in North Carolina's Rural Counties
learning.take: 11 Wake County census tracts meet the USDA food desert definition — the methodology maps directly onto NOURISH and gives us a defensible framework for advocacy.

learning.title: AI-Assisted Crop Disease Classification from Drone Imagery at 94% Accuracy
learning.take: The training pipeline they used for strawberry leaf disease detection is essentially what we explored in the supply chain project — worth cross-referencing.
